snp3.3:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
snp3.3:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
snp3.3:2.1:
“Monks, speech endowed with four characteristics is well-spoken,
not poorly spoken—faultless & not to be faulted by the observant.
snp3.3:2.4:
Speech endowed with these four characteristics is well-spoken,
not poorly spoken—faultless & not to be faulted by the observant.”
snp3.3:2.5: That is what the Blessed One said.
snp3.3:2.6: Having said this, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
snp3.3:3.1: The calm say that what is well-spoken is best;
snp3.3:3.2: second, that one should say what is just, not unjust;
snp3.3:3.4: fourth, what is true, not false.
snp3.3:4.1:
Then Ven. Vaṅgīsa, rising from his seat, arranging his robe over one shoulder,
faced the Blessed One with his hands palm-to-palm in front of his heart and said,
snp3.3:4.2:
“An inspiration has come to me, Blessed One!
An inspiration has come to me, One Well-Gone!”
snp3.3:4.3: “Let the inspiration come to you, Vaṅgīsa,” the Blessed One said.
snp3.3:4.4: Then Ven. Vaṅgīsa praised the Blessed One to his face with these fitting verses:
snp3.3:6.3: Speech when it brings no evil
snp3.3:7.4: —so say the calm— are firmly established on truth.
snp3.3:8.1: The speech the Awakened One speaks,
snp3.3:8.3: for making an end to the mass of stress:
